scope: Bounded names and safety ceilings shared by the dual-attested resource ownership draft family owned by #1070. This file is not a wire schema, a 9P opcode registration, a Privacy Pass token type, an IANA registry, or a codepoint allocation, and it implies no such allocation.

identifiers.note: Stable resource identity, content CID, manifest CID, and operation ID are distinct. No cyclic hash or signature construction is permitted.

lifecycle.note: This logical application label is carried inside registrar and proof-plane records; it is not a registered or mandatory 9P opcode, MoQ track namespace, or Privacy Pass token type.

bounds.note: Repository-local application ceilings. Construction and profile review may lower them; any increase requires a profile revision.

transition_invariants: no final manifest exists without both MAC and ledger attestations over the identical canonical ResourceIntent digest
payment does not confer title and MAC authorization does not prove payment
at most one finalized successor may advance a resource version
every transition carries an expected predecessor or version and is finalized by compare-and-swap with a fencing token
stable resource identity, content CID, manifest CID, and operation ID remain separate
every request and external effect is idempotent by deterministic operation ID
every reservation eventually posts, voids, or enters explicit quarantine or manual review
provisional material is unavailable through the normal namespace
ordinary reads remain MAC-only and ledger or proof-plane I/O is not placed on the read hot path
identified, pairwise, committed-owner, and anonymous-controller or anonymous-payer profiles remain distinct and anonymous authorization never fabricates a DID

effective assurance is the minimum of entitlement, issuer token, binding, MAC decision, ledger attestation, and key-release assurance and classical outer admission cannot raise an inner resource operation
unknown states, versions, profile kinds, or transition events reject
